skill.name: pitch-deck-visuals
skill.description: Investor pitch deck structure with slide-by-slide framework, visual design rules, and data presentation. Covers the 12-slide framework, chart types, team slides, and common investor turn-offs. Use for: fundraising decks, investor presentations, startup pitch, demo day, grant proposals. Triggers: pitch deck, investor deck, startup pitch, fundraising deck, demo day, pitch presentation, investor presentation, seed deck, series a deck, pitch slides, startup presentation, vc pitch, investor meeting
skill.summary: Create investor-ready pitch deck visuals using the inference.sh CLI with the 12-slide framework
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: All 41 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate pitch deck design guide that uses the inference.sh CLI to generate slide visuals. The detected patterns (external_commands, network, sensitive data) are all benign - backticks are markdown code examples, URLs are legitimate service references, and SAM is standard market-sizing terminology (Serviceable Addressable Market), not Windows security databases.

content.value_statement: Generate professional investor pitch deck visuals with the proven 12-slide framework, chart guidelines, and design rules that investors expect.
actual_capabilities: Generate slide backgrounds and visuals using HTML-to-image conversion
Create TAM/SAM/SOM market size charts with concentric circles
Build traction charts showing revenue growth over time
Apply the 12-slide pitch deck framework with timing guidelines
Follow professional typography, color, and layout rules
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and authentication
Does not generate actual business content - only visual templates
No built-in data sourcing - users must provide their own metrics
Image generation depends on inference.sh service availability

use_cases.title: Startup Fundraising
use_cases.description: Create investor-ready pitch decks for seed or Series A rounds with professional visuals that follow venture capital expectations
use_cases.target_user: Startup founders preparing for investor meetings

use_cases.title: Demo Day Preparation
use_cases.description: Generate polished presentation visuals for accelerator demo days with concise, impactful slides

use_cases.title: Grant Applications
use_cases.description: Build persuasive visual narratives for grant proposals requiring clear problem-solution framing

prompt_templates.title: Basic Slide Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pitch-deck-visuals skill to create a title slide for my startup [Company Name] with tagline [tagline]. Generate the HTML for a dark-themed professional slide.

prompt_templates.title: Market Size Chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pitch-deck-visuals to create a TAM/SAM/SOM market size visualization with TAM=$50B, SAM=$8B, SOM=$800M. Generate the Python code for matplotlib.

prompt_templates.title: Traction Chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a traction slide showing monthly revenue growth from $8K to $120K over 8 months. Use the pitch-deck-visuals style guide with the brand color #818cf8.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Positioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a 2x2 competitive positioning map comparing us against [Competitor A], [Competitor B], and manual solutions. Label axes as Manual to Automated and Basic to Advanced features.

output_examples.input: Generate a problem slide showing that teams waste 12 hours per week on manual reporting
output_examples.output: A 1920x1080 slide with dark navy background (#0f0f23), large amber text showing '12 hrs/week' at 144px, supporting problem statement, and source citation from Forrester Research
Concentric circle chart with TAM (outer, $50B), SAM (middle, $8B), and SOM (inner, $800M) in brand colors on dark background

best_practices: Follow the 1-6-6 rule: 1 idea per slide, 6 words max per bullet, 6 bullets max per slide
Use the 12-slide framework with the ~6 minute total timing guideline
Cite data sources on every slide to build credibility with investors
anti_patterns: Using pie charts - they are hard to read and unprofessional in pitch decks
Creating feature comparison tables against competitors - use 2x2 positioning maps instead
Including more than 4 people on the team slide - appears unfocused

faq.question: What tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: This skill uses the inference.sh CLI to generate slide visuals. You need to install it first using curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh

faq.question: How many slides should a pitch deck have?
faq.answer: Aim for 12 slides maximum. The total presentation should take about 6 minutes. Never exceed 20 slides.

faq.question: What is TAM/SAM/SOM?
faq.answer: TAM (Total Addressable Market) is the total market demand. SAM (Serviceable Addressable Market) is your target segment. SOM (Serviceable Obtainable Market) is your realistic near-term capture.

faq.question: Can I use this for grant proposals?
faq.answer: Yes, the visual framework works for grant applications. Focus on problem-solution framing and impact metrics.

faq.question: What colors should I use?
faq.answer: Use either dark (navy, charcoal) or clean white backgrounds. Pick one and stick with it. Use one brand accent color for emphasis.

faq.question: How do I show traction to investors?
faq.answer: Use up-and-to-the-right line charts showing revenue or user growth over time. Include the key metric as a callout with the growth percentage.
